name: Magnite CTV and Programmatic Ad Platform API
what_it_does: Independent supply-side platform (SSP) with REST API — formed from Rubicon Project + Telaria + SpotX + SpringServe mergers, specializing in CTV/streaming video monetization, programmatic display, and publisher ad management, with private marketplace capabilities and sophisticated audience data tools for premium publishers.
use_cases: Managing programmatic demand connections and PMP deals for publisher inventory via Magnite API
Querying CTV ad revenue and streaming monetization metrics for publisher reporting
Configuring SpringServe ad server settings for streaming video publishers via API
Managing audience segments and first-party data activation for premium inventory
Accessing Magnite's real-time bidding marketplace data for yield optimization
not_for: Advertiser campaign management (Magnite is publisher-side SSP — use DSPs for buying)
Small publishers (<5M monthly impressions) — Magnite focuses on premium publishers
Social platform advertising (Magnite serves open internet publishers, not walled gardens)
best_when: A premium digital publisher or streaming video platform needs an independent CTV/streaming SSP partner with deep programmatic infrastructure — especially for connected TV content publishers seeking premium programmatic revenue.
avoid_when: You're a small publisher, advertiser rather than publisher, or primarily monetize through direct sales without programmatic demand.

auth.notes: OAuth2 client credentials for API access. API key for legacy endpoints (Rubicon Project legacy API). Bearer token auth. Publisher-scoped access. No webhooks — must poll for reporting. SpringServe (ad server) has separate API. CTV monetization via Magnite CTV API. Deal management via Deal API. Multiple legacy systems from mergers — Rubicon, SpotX, SpringServe each had separate APIs.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier; revenue share model
pricing.paid_tiers: Revenue share on monetized impressions; contact sales
pricing.notes: Los Angeles company. Formed from multiple mergers: Rubicon Project + Telaria (2020), SpotX (2021), SpringServe (2021). NASDAQ listed (MGNI). $600M+ revenue (2023). CTV/streaming focus is key differentiator — #1 independent CTV SSP. SpringServe acquisition brought streaming ad server capability. 1,000+ premium publisher customers. Disney, Fox, Univision use Magnite for streaming monetization. Competing directly with Google's Ad Manager for premium video publishers seeking independence.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API for inventory, deals, reporting, and SpringServe ad server management. OAuth2 and API key auth. Documentation fragmented across legacy products (Rubicon, SpotX, SpringServe) and unified Magnite docs. CTV API separate from display API. No webhooks. Multiple legacy systems from acquisitions create API complexity. Dedicated publisher success team for enterprise accounts.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Reporting reads idempotent. Deal reads idempotent. SpringServe configuration updates use versioning.
known_agent_gotchas: Multiple legacy APIs from acquisitions (Rubicon, SpotX, SpringServe) — documentation fragmented
CTV monetization uses SpringServe API — separate from display programmatic API
TCF2 consent management required for EU programmatic monetization
PMP deal setup is multi-party — buyer DSP agreement required before revenue flows
Multiple ownership/merger history — validate which API endpoints are current vs deprecated

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. GDPR, CCPA, TCF2 compliant. US and EU data residency. OAuth2 with scopes. NASDAQ-listed with public company security standards. Publisher revenue data highly sensitive — strict API access controls. TCF2 consent chain required for EU programmatic. CTV viewer data subject to Video Privacy Protection Act (VPPA) — implement careful data handling.
